---
title: "Research"
---

## Overview

My research lies in algebraic geometry and number theory, broadly construed. I am interested in geometric objects that arise naturally in arithmetic contexts and in the algebraic, cohomological, and representation-theoretic structures attached to them.

A recurring theme in my work is the use of geometric methods to understand arithmetic phenomena.

## Research Themes

### Algebraic Geometry

I am interested in the geometry of algebraic varieties, especially moduli spaces and their cohomological invariants.

Topics of interest include:

- Moduli problems
- Algebraic curves
- Vector bundles and sheaves
- Intersection theory
- Derived and homological methods

### Number Theory

I am interested in arithmetic questions that can be studied using geometric methods.

Topics of interest include:

- Arithmetic geometry
- Galois representations
- Rational points
- Arithmetic moduli spaces
- Étale cohomology

### Representation Theory

I am also interested in the role of representation theory in geometry and number theory.

Topics of interest include:

- Representations of algebraic groups
- Automorphic forms
- Geometric representation theory
- Hecke operators
- Symmetry in cohomological problems

## Current Projects

### Project One

Brief high-level description of the project. You do not need to reveal technical details of unpublished work.

### Project Two

Brief high-level description of another current or future project.

### Project Three

Brief high-level description of a possible expository or collaborative project.

## Collaborators

- Collaborator Name, Institution
- Collaborator Name, Institution

## Research Statement

A research statement will be available here when appropriate.

<!-- 
You can later add:
[Download research statement](files/research-statement.pdf)
-->

## Research Papers

### Title of Your Research Paper

With Coauthor Name. Preprint, 2026.

**Abstract.**  
We study a class of mathematical objects arising in algebraic geometry and number theory. Our main result establishes a structural theorem relating their cohomology to naturally occurring representation-theoretic data.

**Links:**  
[PDF](#) | [arXiv](#) | [Slides](#)

<details>
<summary>BibTeX</summary>

```bibtex
@misc{yourname2026paper,
  title        = {Title of Your Research Paper},
  author       = {Your Name and Coauthor Name},
  year         = {2026},
  eprint       = {0000.00000},
  archivePrefix = {arXiv},
  primaryClass = {math.AG}
}
